content: Define the TopModule with its interface including clk, load, ena, data inputs, and q output. Specify the bit widths for each port where applicable.
source:
input  clk
input  load
input  ena  (2 bits)
input  data (100 bits)
output q    (100 bits)

content: Implement a 100-bit register to store the data input. This register should load the new data when the load signal is high, otherwise it should retain its previous value. The register update should be triggered on the positive edge of the clk.
source:
input  clk
input  load
input  data (100 bits)

content: Design the logic to determine the direction of rotation based on the 2-bit ena input. Use combinational logic to set the direction to either left or right.

Implement the rotation logic for the 100-bit data stored in the register. The rotation should be either left or right based on the direction determined by the ena input. Ensure the rotation logic minimizes critical path delay by using efficient combinational logic structures.
source:
input  ena  (2 bits)
input  data (100 bits)

content: Connect the output of the rotation logic to the q output port of the module. This connection should be made using structural Verilog code.
